Itching.description: Itching is an irritating sensation that causes a desire to scratch the skin. It can be caused by allergies, dry skin, or infections.

dosage.Calamine lotion: Apply 2-3 times daily on affected area.
dosage.Hydrocortisone cream: Apply thinly once or twice a day for up to 7 days.
dosage.Antihistamines: Take one tablet (10 mg) daily before bedtime.

Skin Rash.description: A skin rash is an area of irritated or swollen skin, often due to allergies, infections, or autoimmune conditions.

dosage.Hydrocortisone: Apply twice daily to the rash.

Nodal Skin Eruptions.description: These are inflamed, painful nodules or lumps under the skin, commonly caused by infections or immune responses.

dosage.Antibiotics: 500 mg twice a day for 5–7 days.
dosage.NSAIDs: Take 1 tablet (400 mg) after meals, twice daily.

Continuous Sneezing.description: A reflex reaction to irritants in the nasal passages, often due to allergies or infections.
precautions: Avoid allergens like dust and pollen.

dosage.Antihistamines: Take as per severity, generally 1 tablet/day.

Shivering.description: Shivering is a bodily response to cold or fever, often indicating infection or inflammation.

dosage.Paracetamol: 500 mg every 6 hours if fever persists.

Chills.description: Chills often accompany fever and involve feeling cold and shivering, typically from infection.
precautions: Stay wrapped in warm clothing or blankets.
Check for fever and manage it.

dosage.Paracetamol: Take 500 mg every 4–6 hours as needed.
dosage.Blankets: Use continuously until body temperature normalizes.

Joint Pain.description: Joint pain may result from inflammation, injury, or chronic conditions like arthritis.

Stomach Pain.description: Pain in the stomach area may be due to indigestion, infection, or ulcers.

dosage.Dicyclomine: 10 mg 3 times a day before meals.
dosage.Antacids: 1/2 tablets after meals and before bedtime.

Acidity.description: Acidity occurs when stomach acid flows back into the esophagus, causing heartburn and discomfort.
precautions: Reduce intake of caffeine and spicy food.
Don't lie down immediately after eating.

Ulcers on Tongue.description: Painful sores in the mouth, often caused by stress, minor injury, or certain foods.

dosage.Topical gel: Apply 3–4 times a day on ulcers.

Muscle Wasting.description: Loss of muscle mass due to lack of use, disease, or malnutrition.

dosage.Nutritional supplements: 1 sachet daily with milk or water.

Vomiting.description: Vomiting is the act of ejecting stomach contents, often due to infection, food poisoning, or motion sickness.
precautions: Avoid solid food until vomiting stops.

dosage.Domperidone: 10 mg before meals up to 3 times daily.

Burning Micturition.description: A burning sensation while urinating, often due to urinary tract infection (UTI).

Spotting Urination.description: Small amounts of blood or unusual spotting during urination, possibly indicating infection or trauma.

dosage.Antibiotics: As prescribed, commonly 500 mg twice daily.

Fatigue.description: A feeling of tiredness or lack of energy, often linked to overwork, stress, or illness.

Weight Gain.description: An increase in body weight that may result from diet, hormonal imbalance, or medical conditions.

dosage.Thyroxine: 25–100 mcg daily on empty stomach (if needed).
dosage.Metformin: 500 mg once or twice daily with meals.

Anxiety.description: A mental health condition marked by excessive worry, restlessness, or nervousness.

Cold Hands and Feets.description: Reduced blood circulation or cold weather can cause extremities to feel cold frequently.

Mood Swings.description: Rapid changes in mood often linked to hormonal changes, stress, or mental health conditions.

Weight Loss.description: Unintentional weight loss may indicate underlying illness, malnutrition, or metabolic issues.

dosage.Appetite stimulants: As prescribed, usually once daily before meals.

Fungal infection.description: A fungal infection affects the skin, nails, or moist body areas and can cause itching, redness, scaling, or ring-shaped rashes.

dosage.Clotrimazole cream: Apply thinly twice daily for 2-4 weeks.
dosage.Antifungal powder: Dust affected area once or twice daily.
dosage.Cetirizine: 10 mg once daily at night if itching is troublesome.

precautions: Keep the area clean and dry.
Continue treatment for the advised duration.
Fungal infection.advice: This is AI-assisted guidance. Consult a qualified doctor for confirmation, prescriptions, severe symptoms, pregnancy, children, elderly patients, or long-lasting illness.
